frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Sach bolun, maa ke haath ki simple dal roti mein jo sukoon hota hai na, woh five-star khane mein bhi nahi milta. Puri zindagi ghar ko sambhala, kabhi hisaab nahi rakha, bas pyaar se sabko khilaya. Aaj kal ki bahuon ko bhi samajhna chahiye ki ghar ka asli taste aur barkat kitchen se hi aati hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it glorifies a mother’s unpaid domestic labor as pure love and sets it up as the standard women, specifically daughters-in-law, should meet. It encodes nostalgic praise of 'maa ke haath ka khana' along with an implicit expectation that a good woman proves her value through cooking and home-making.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 41. Divorced two years ago. A Punjabi man working as a real estate developer in Chandigarh. You are being interviewed on a men's rights YouTube channel with 200000 subscribers. The host asks you to share your divorce story and what men need to know about Indian marriage laws. You are speaking under your real name and telling your story in a way that frames your ex wife as having planned the divorce from the beginning to take your money and property.
